text.title: roadmap-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for roadmap-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Feature list with specific dates for every item.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dates imply commitment. When any date slips, stakeholder trust erodes. Use confidence levels instead of dates for everything beyond "Now".

failure-mode.description: No strategic connection — initiatives not linked to objectives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Orphaned initiatives accumulate and eventually dominate the roadmap without anyone being able to justify why. Every initiative must have an objective ID.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If items never graduate from Later to Next, stakeholders stop believing the roadmap is real. Review Later quarterly and either promote, archive, or move to not-doing with a reason.

failure-mode.description: Same roadmap shared with engineering, exec, and customers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each audience needs a different abstraction level. Engineering needs initiative detail; exec needs theme-level strategy; customers need benefit language with no internal jargon.
